title: Garrett J. Knowlton
text:
I am a graduate student at the University of Wisconsin-Madison in the Department of Integrative Biology. I am broadly interested in landscape ecology, ecological modeling, data science, and climate change science and adaptation.


University of Wisconsin-Madison | Madison, WI | MS in Integrative Biology (Ecology) | August 2022 - Present
Michigan State University | East Lansing, MI | B.S in Fisheries & Wildlife Biology | Aug 2015 - May 2020



Landscape and Ecosystem Ecology Lab (Advised by Dr. Monica Turner) | NSF Graduate Research Fellow | August 2022 - present
U.S. National Park Service, Climate Change Response Program | Research Assistant | August 2021 - September 2022
NASA DEVELOP | Research Assistant | Jan 2021 - May 2021

title: About me
text: My research broadly focuses on using simulation models and machine learning to understand how subalpine forests might change in the northern Rocky Mountains, USA in response to changing climate and fire regimes.
